sections.paragraph:
μειόω 1 aor. ἐμείωσα (s. μείωσις; X., Pla. et al.; Strabo: 91 Fgm. 18 Jac. [Jos., Ant. 15, 10]; PFay 26, 15 [II a.d.] al. in pap; Sir 43:7; Philo, Rer. Div. Her. 140; SibOr 12, 134; Ar. 6, 2; Ath., R. 7 p. 56, 7) lessen τὶ someth. τὸ σεμνὸν τῆς φιλαδελφίας ὑμῶν the respect that is due your brotherly love 1 Cl 47:5 (cp. SIG 783, 8f οὐκ ἐμίωσέν τι τῆς πατρῴας ἀρετῆς).—DELG s.v. μείων.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 626
